name: FutureHouse Platform Agents
aliases: FutureHouse Platform: Superintelligent AI Agents for Scientific Discovery
current_status: Active public science product suite with web interface and API
boundary_notes: Included because it offers a suite of specialized agents for literature search, synthesis, chemistry planning, and data analysis through a product platform. It is broader than a single research assistant and narrower than a fully autonomous end-to-end scientist.
execution_environment: Product-hosted environment accessible through a web interface and API
human_roles: Researchers choose the agent, provide the scientific question, interpret results, and decide whether to act on outputs. Downstream experiments and scientific decisions remain human responsibilities.
autonomy_rationale: The platform automates specialized research subtasks with strong retrieval and synthesis capabilities, but it does not independently run a full closed-loop discovery process without user orchestration or later composition into a system such as Robin.
iteration_loop: Limited. Individual agents support iterative querying and source review, and the platform can be chained by users, but the launch announcement does not present a fully autonomous end-to-end research loop.
stopping_and_guardrails: Guardrails described in the launch materials include transparent reasoning, source-quality evaluation, human-facing delivery through a web interface and API, and an explicit warning that Phoenix is experimental and may make more mistakes.
known_failure_modes: overreliance on platform outputs without experimental review
benchmarks_used: head-to-head literature search tasks against PhD-level researchers
retrieval precision and accuracy comparisons against frontier search models
metrics: head-to-head literature search precision versus PhD-level researchers
baselines: major frontier search models with a search tool
PhD-level researchers in literature search tasks
evaluation_limitations: The launch post states that Phoenix is less deeply benchmarked than the other agents, and the reviewed sources do not provide a single unified benchmark covering every agent in the suite.
paper_url: No single paper URL is provided for the full platform launch in the reviewed primary sources [uncertain]
code_url: No single official open-source repository for the full platform suite is provided in the reviewed primary sources [uncertain]
dataset_url: No dedicated dataset URL is provided for the full suite in the reviewed primary sources [uncertain]
leaderboard_url: No standalone public leaderboard URL is linked in the reviewed primary sources [uncertain]
predecessors: earlier FutureHouse literature agents and evaluation work [uncertain]
evaluates: Not a benchmark; it does not primarily evaluate other systems.
milestones: 2025-05-01: public web interface and API announced
2025-05-20: Robin announcement described Crow, Falcon, Owl, Phoenix, and Finch as specialized agents feeding a unified workflow
version_history: Initial launch with Crow, Falcon, Owl, and Phoenix on 2025-05-01
Finch publicly referenced as a specialized data-analysis agent by 2025-05-20
No formal versioned release history was provided in the reviewed primary sources [uncertain]
adoption_signals: Public launch with free access via web interface at announcement time
API availability announced for researcher workflows
FutureHouse positioned the suite as publicly available to scientists everywhere
supersession_notes: Not superseded in the reviewed primary sources. Robin is presented as a downstream orchestration of specialized platform agents rather than a replacement for the suite.
deployment_constraints: human review for chemistry and downstream experimental decisions
cost_and_runtime: The launch announcement said researchers could try the platform for free via the web interface. The longer-term pricing or credit model for broader API usage is not fully specified in the reviewed primary sources [uncertain].
scientific_integrity_risks: source-quality errors that shape scientific conclusions
benchmark overclaiming from narrow task coverage
security_and_safety_risks: unsafe downstream action on unverified agent advice
platform concentration risk if users overtrust hosted agents
mitigations: benchmarking against frontier models and human experts
explicit warning that Phoenix is experimental
policy_relevance: The platform is relevant to governance because it operationalizes high-capability scientific agents behind a hosted interface and API, raising issues around access control, evidence standards, chemical dual use, and responsible deployment.
